pattern: Every letter that shows up more than once can always be collapsed down to a single surviving copy, so the true floor on the string's length is just how many distinct letters it contains.
hints: Think about what happens to a letter that appears three or four times in the string versus a letter that appears only once -- does the final length care how many times a letter repeats, or only whether it appears at all?
Try counting each letter of the alphabet that shows up in the string at least once, ignoring how many times it repeats.
The answer is exactly the number of distinct letters present in the string -- no simulation of the removal steps is needed.

edge_inputs.description: mixed repeats, one letter appearing once
all 26 letters distinct, no repeats to collapse
one letter repeated many times collapses to a single survivor
already fully distinct, answer equals the full length

stress.description: random lowercase strings of varying length and letter diversity
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    alphabet_size = rng.randint(1, 26)
    letters = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz'[:alphabet_size]
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(letters) for _ in range(n))
    return [s]
